notes: Diogenes of Sinope, Cynic philosopher known for his ascetic lifestyle and sharp wit.

docs.title: Life of Sulla
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: And the archers, being hard pressed by the Romans, and wanting space for a retreat, took their arrows by handfuls, and striking with these as with swords, beat them back.

docs.title: CHAPTER XVI
docs.author: Procopius
docs.excerpt: … a native of Constantinople, an agreeable person, who was liked by the Emperor and everyone else. In her wrath, she accused him, in like manner, of sodomy, and, having suborned two of his servants, put them up to give evidence against and to accuse their master.

docs.title: Letter 131
docs.author: Synesius of Cyrene
docs.excerpt: He is a loyal young man, noble, both gentle and brave — exactly the kind of man Plato would have wanted as a guardian in his republic. He saw military service while still a youth.
